name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value larger than the original input

name: align_to_one_changes_value
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_to_one_changes_value(a: usize)
    requires
        a <= usize::MAX,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, 1usize) != a,
{
}
reason: Every integer is a multiple of 1, so aligning to 1 must be a no-op

name: align_result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_3_align_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        b <= a,
    ensures
        !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: The whole point of align_to_usize is to produce a b-aligned result; failing that breaks its contract

name: zero_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_4_zero_not_aligned(size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(0nat, size),
{
}
reason: Zero is divisible by every positive integer, so aligned(0, n) must be true for all n > 0

name: aligned_implies_align_noop_fails
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_implies_align_noop_fails(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a as nat, b as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: If a is already aligned to b then align_to_usize(a,b) must equal a; the contrary would mean alignment is lossy
